From Your Coach
Nick,
Let's Get It.
Real Talk
You already know me better than anyone so I'm not gonna sit here and sell you on who I am. You've seen the work firsthand. You know what I've been through and you know I don't put my name on anything I don't fully believe in. That's exactly why I took the time to build this properly for you — because you deserve a real plan, not some generic bullshit someone copy-pasted off the internet.
Why I Built This For You
You asked me for help and I take that seriously. This plan is built specifically around your body, your schedule, your injuries, and your goals. Every single detail in here — the workout split, the nutrition, the supplements — is dialed in for YOU. Not a template. Not a cookie cutter. Yours.
What I Need From You
I need you to show up. That's it. You don't have to be perfect — nobody is. But I need you consistent. Hit that incline walk every morning even when you don't want to. Log your food. Drink your water. Check in with me. The days you don't feel like it are the days that matter the most. I've been at 278 lbs. I know what it takes to flip the switch. Now it's your turn.
I Got You
You're not doing this alone. I'm here the whole way — adjusting the plan as you progress, answering questions, keeping you accountable. We're gonna get you to 209 and then we're gonna keep going. Let's make it happen starting tomorrow. Love you bro — now go get it.
Start Date: March 24
Goal: 209 lbs
Coach: Jack Wayne
No Excuses
Client Overview
Your
Plan
Coach's Note: Losing 21 lbs in one month is extremely aggressive. Healthy, sustainable fat loss is 1–2 lbs per week — meaning 4–8 lbs in month one is a genuine win. We push hard, but smart. Stay consistent and the results will compound fast.
Profile
Goal230 → 209 lbs
Start DateMarch 24, 2026
Timeline8–12 weeks
Gym Schedule6:30 – 8:45 AM
Tracking ToolGarmin Watch + Calorie App
Injuries & Limitations
Lower BackAvoid heavy compound loading
AchillesNo heavy squats or lunges
StomachDairy sensitivity — yogurt OK in moderation
Weekly Structure
MondayDay 1 — Chest & Triceps
TuesdayDay 2 — Back & Biceps
WednesdayDay 3 — Shoulders, Legs & Cardio
ThursdayDay 1 — Chest & Triceps
FridayDay 2 — Back & Biceps
SaturdayDay 3 — Shoulders, Legs & Cardio
SundayCardio / Yoga / Recovery
Rest DaysOnly if your body tells you — listen to it
Fuel Protocol
Nutrition
Plan
<2,200
Calories / Day
160g+
Protein / Day
<40g
Carbs / Day
Breakfast — Optional
2–3 eggs over easy, scrambled, or hard boiled
Scrambled egg whites with salt and pepper
Oikos Triple Zero yogurt with blueberries (dairy tolerated in small amounts)
Lunch
4–6 oz protein: chicken, salmon, bison, turkey, or beef
Large salad with balsamic or hot sauce dressing
Snacks: almonds, pistachios, Quest chips
Hydration: half gallon of water by lunchtime
Pre-Workout Snack
Banana OR 1–2 tbsp natural peanut butter
2 hard boiled eggs
Dinner
Ground turkey in lettuce cups with stir-fry or hot sauce
Bison burger (no bun) with pepper jack cheese
Any approved protein with salad or roasted vegetables
After Dinner — Optional
Yogurt with light fruit and cinnamon
Keto friendly protein bar
Watermelon or pineapple (small portion)
Natural peanut butter (1–2 tbsp)
Cottage cheese — if tolerated well
Parmesan Reggiano — small amount as savory snack
Rules
Water1 gallon minimum daily
AlcoholTequila or vodka soda only if drinking (try not to drink Nick 😂)
Cheat mealsTry not to cheat — it will derail everything. You will hit a flow state if you remove junk from your diet.
Progress pictureTake one every day — even if you don't want to.
Sleep8 hrs ideal. No food before bed.
After two weeks on this plan you won't crave sugar or processed food anymore. Your body adapts fast. Protein becomes the fuel that keeps the machine running.
Caffeine: Caffeine is your friend. Use it. Pre-workout, black coffee, whatever works for you — it'll sharpen your focus and push your performance. Sometimes we overdo it, that's fine — just make sure to hydrate.
Training Program
Workout
Split
Daily Non-Negotiables
Daily Walk30–45 min walk every day — separate from your workout
Incline WalkEvery single day — Incline 12 / Speed 3–3.5
Duration15–30 minutes
AbsCable crunches / abs of your choice — end of every session
RecoverySauna or cold shower post-workout
ElectrolytesLMNT during every workout
Schedule: Mon → Day 1 · Tue → Day 2 · Wed → Day 3 · Thu → Day 1 · Fri → Day 2 · Sat → Day 3 · Sun → Cardio/Yoga. No scheduled rest days — but if your body is telling you something, listen to it. That's not weakness, that's smart training.
Day 1 — Chest & Triceps
Incline Treadmill Walk15–30 minIncline 12 / Speed 3–3.5
Flat Bench Press (45s each side)3 × 10Go heavier once comfortable or have a spotter
Incline Bench Press (Smith Machine)3 × 10Controlled movement
Cable Chest Flys (mid)3 × 10+ 10 sled pushes each set
Low Cable Chest Flys3 × 10Alternating arms
Tricep Pushdowns (cable)3 × 10Full extension
Tricep Kickbacks (cable)3 × 10Controlled tempo
Skull Crushers (EZ bar)3 × 10+ 10 EZ bar press, elbows tucked
Push-ups between setsTo failureIntensity booster
Cable Crunches3–5 × 15Facing the stack
Optional: 30-sec Sprints2–3 setsEnd of cardio for extra burn
Day 2 — Back & Biceps Full stretch on every pull
Incline Treadmill Walk15–30 minIncline 12 / Speed 3–3.5
Lat Pulldowns (wide grip)3 × 10Full stretch at top
Seated Cable Rows3 × 10Squeeze at peak contraction
Cable Hammer Curls (rope)3 × 10Neutral grip
Preacher Curls3 × 10Slow negative
Dumbbell Curls3 × 10Alternate arms
Back Extensions3 × 10Light weight — protect lower back
Rear Delt Machine Flys3 × 10Full range of motion
Cable Crunches3–5 × 15Facing the stack
Optional: 30-sec Sprints2–3 setsEnd of cardio for extra burn
Day 3 — Shoulders, Legs & Cardio Low impact — protect Achilles
Incline Treadmill Walk15–30 minIncline 12 / Speed 3–3.5
DB Side Lateral Raises3 × 10Lightweight, strict form
DB Front Lateral Raises3 × 10Controlled
Smith Machine Shoulder Press3 × 10Increase weight gradually
Leg Press (machine)3 × 12No heavy squats — protect Achilles
Leg Extensions (machine)3 × 12Controlled movement
Seated Leg Curl (machine)3 × 12Avoid heavy Achilles load
Ab Circuit (mats)2× / weekPlanks, leg raises, obliques
Optional: 30-sec Sprints2–3 setsEnd of cardio for extra burn
Sunday — Cardio & Recovery Reset for the week
Yoga or Mobility Session30–60 minFlexibility & recovery focus
Light Cardio / Outdoor Walk20–30 minStay moving
